title: Form PF; Reporting Requirements for All Filers
raw_text:
Form PF; Reporting Requirements for All Filers

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (the "CFTC") and the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") (collectively, "we" or the "Commissions") are proposing to amend Form PF, the confidential reporting form for certain SEC-registered investment advisers to private funds, including those that also are registered with the CFTC as a commodity pool operator (a "CPO") or a commodity trading advisor (a "CTA"). The proposed amendments would eliminate certain filing and reporting obligations, streamline certain requirements, and make corrections and other revisions. The proposed amendments are designed to eliminate certain burdens, among other things.

title: Concept Release on Consolidated Audit Trail and Other Audit Trails and Data Sources
raw_text:
Concept Release on Consolidated Audit Trail and Other Audit Trails and Data Sources

The Securities and Exchange Commission (the "Commission") is publishing this concept release to solicit comments in support of a comprehensive review of the Consolidated Audit Trail and other audit trails and related data sources currently used in the regulation of U.S. securities markets, including comments regarding the funding mechanisms for these audit trails and/or related data sources. There have been several developments since the Commission last evaluated the scope and sufficiency of these audit trails and related data sources. These developments have prompted the Commission to consider whether changes should be made to the rules and regulations governing existing audit trails and related data sources to better respond to and reflect current market conditions; demonstrated regulatory needs; civil liberty, privacy, and confidentiality concerns; cost-efficient technology solutions; and cybersecurity considerations.

title: MIAX Sapphire Exchange - May 1, 2026 Fee Changes
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Fee Change Alert — MIAX Sapphire Exchange - May 1, 2026 Fee Changes
Fee Change Alert MIAX Sapphire April 30, 2026 | 03:03 PM MIAX Sapphire Exchange - May 1, 2026 Fee Changes Effective May 1, 2026, MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange will amend the following fees and rebates, pending filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission: Introduction of MIAX Sapphire Monthly Trading Floor Firm Fee Cap Floor fees for eligible Firm-Origin Qualified Floor Orders (QFO), Complex Qualified Floor Orders (cQFO), Qualified Contingent Cross (QCC) transactions, and Complex Qualified Contingent Cross (cQCC) transactions will be capped at $225,000 per month per eligible participant OCC clearing account Fees for Strategy Trades executed on the MIAX Sapphire Trading Floor and Electronic transactions are excluded Transactions in commingled clearing accounts are not eligible, and not subject to the Monthly Trading Floor Firm Fee Cap Floor Broker Rebates will be adjusted once an eligible participant clearing account reaches the Monthly Trading Floor Firm Fee Cap QFO/cQFQ - Floor Broker Rebates will not be paid on non-billable (capped) sides Floor QCC/cQCC – Floor Broker rebates will be adjusted and eligible for rebates of ($0.00)/($0.07)/($0.17) depending on the origins on the transactions Attached is a highlighted summary of the fee changes. Complete details will be contained in the May 1, 2026 exchange fee schedules, when posted on the MIAX website at MIAX Sapphire Fee Schedule . For additional information, please contact MIAX Sales at Sales@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-8177. For assistance, please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. MIAX Sapphire Highlight of Fee Changes 05012026

raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - New Listing Effective for May 1, 2026
Listing Alert MIAX Emerald April 30, 2026 | 01:39 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - New Listing Effective for May 1, 2026 The following option class will begin trading on the MIAX Options Exchange, MIAX Pearl Options Exchange, MIAX Emerald Options Exchange and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange on Friday, May 1, 2026: Pershing Square Inc. (PS) Market Makers can use the Member Firm Portal (MFP) to manage their option class assignments. All LMM and RMM Option Class Assignments must be entered prior to 6:00 PM ET on the business day immediately preceding the effective date. title: MIAX Sapphire Exchange - April 22, 2026 Fee Changes
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Fee Change Alert — MIAX Sapphire Exchange - April 22, 2026 Fee Changes
Fee Change Alert MIAX Sapphire April 22, 2026 | 09:27 AM MIAX Sapphire Exchange - April 22, 2026 Fee Changes Effective April 22, 2026, the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange has amended the following fees and rebates: MIAX Sapphire Floor Transactions The surcharge for certain Customer Complex Qualified Floor Order (“cQFO”) transactions (“Complex Surcharge”) and Floor Broker Enhanced Complex Rebate are discontinued as of April 22, 2026. Floor transactions that incurred the Complex Surcharge fee and Floor Broker Enhanced Rebate between April 1, 2026 and April 21, 2026 will remain effective. Attached is a highlighted summary of the fee changes. Complete details will be contained in the April 2026 exchange fee schedules, when posted on the MIAX website at MIAX Sapphire Fee Schedule . For additional information, please contact MIAX Sales at Sales@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-8177. For assistance, please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. MIAX Sapphire Highlight of Fee Changes 04222026

title: MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Rescheduled: Planned network switch refresh, upgrade for 10G ULL extranet switches and mandatory testing requirements
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Rescheduled: Planned network switch refresh, upgrade for 10G ULL extranet switches and mandatory testing requirements
Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Emerald April 20, 2026 | 02:02 PM MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Rescheduled: Planned network switch refresh, upgrade for 10G ULL extranet switches and mandatory testing requirements Originally announced in the February 13, 2026 Alert , the upgrade of the MIAX Emerald Options 10G ULL extranet switches and increase of the number of available customer-facing edge switches from six to ten has been rescheduled to Friday, November 6, 2026 (previously June 12, 2026) post close of trading. Existing 10G ULL extranet switches are approaching vendor end of support and require replacement. Existing firm cross connects will be migrated by MIAX on the cutover date to the six upgraded switches. For the four additional extranet edge switches, if firms want cross-connect (CC) activation to coincide with the upgrade, they must submit LOAs at least four weeks prior to the Friday, November 6, 2026 cutover date. Following the upgrade, firms must conduct mandatory testing : Mandatory testing will be held on Saturday, November 7, 2026 from 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. ET. Firms are required to perform the following verifications: Verify all 10G ULL CCs by logging into each subscribed application for each CC (i.e. establish application-level socket connections to MEO, MEI, FOI, CTD, etc., for all ports, across all CCs) and verify the login. Verify A/B feed heartbeats for all subscribed multicast feeds on each CC. After verifying connectivity, firms must send an email to TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com and receive confirmation testing was completed successfully. Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to arrange testing or to receive additional information on the planned network refresh.

title: MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Updated FOI Interface Specification to Support Upcoming Increased Options Leg Ratio Enhancement
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Updated FOI Interface Specification to Support Upcoming Increased Options Leg Ratio Enhancement
Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Sapphire April 15, 2026 | 03:58 PM MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Updated FOI Interface Specification to Support Upcoming Increased Options Leg Ratio Enhancement The MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange FIX Order Interface (FOI) Specification has been updated to support the increased Options Leg Ratio Functionality enhancement. The FOI Interface Specification v2.3a has been updated as follows: “LegRatioQty (tag 623)” for an individual Option leg can be up to 999,999 in the following messages New Order – Multileg (MsgType = AB) New Order Cross – Multileg (MsgType = As) Order Cancel/Replace – Multileg (MsgType = AC) Updated specifications can be found at MIAX Sapphire Options Interface Specifications Important Dates: An activation schedule for these changes will be announced in a future alert These changes will be available in the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange's Firm Test Bed (FTB2) environment beginning April 16, 2026 Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to update configurations, arrange interface testing and schedule certification .

raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Technical Alert, Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options & Equities Markets - Reg SCI / SIFMA BCP Testing on Saturday, October 24, 2026
Technical Alert, Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Sapphire April 15, 2026 | 10:04 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options & Equities Markets - Reg SCI / SIFMA BCP Testing on Saturday, October 24, 2026 Overview: The MIAX Exchange Group will participate in the 2026 SIFMA BCP Testing on Saturday, October 24, 2026. All Members that are required to test with MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, MIAX Sapphire Options and/or MIAX Pearl Equities Exchanges in accordance with Regulation Systems Compliance and Integrity (Regulation SCI) have been notified . However, all Members are encouraged to test. All Exchange designated Members are required to maintain connection to the MIAX Disaster Recovery (DR) environment for a 12-month period after receiving notification for mandatory testing. All Exchanges will operate out of the Chicago (CH4) DR site during the scheduled test. Designation Standards - Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plans Testing Member: MIAX Options Exchange MIAX_Options_RC_2025-107 MIAX PEARL Options Exchange MIAX_PEARL_RC_2025-109 MIAX Emerald Options Exchange MIAX_Emerald_RC_2025-106 MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange MIAX_Sapphire_RC_2025-129 MIAX PEARL Equities Exchange MIAX_PEARL_Equities_RC_2025-14 For test day support, to coordinate participation or to arrange connectivity testing, please contact: MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 Regulatory inquiries should be directed to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609)-897-7309 Pre-Testing: Availability to the DR environments for each of the MIAX Exchanges will be available: Saturday, September 26, 2026, 8:00 a.m. – 12:00 p.m. EST Application layer connectivity testing Saturday, October 10, 2026, 8:00 a.m. – 12:00 p.m. EST Application layer connectivity testing Monday, October 19, 2026 through Friday, October 23, 2026, 8:00 26-P-260.00 BAC–Jan 15 27-C-50.00 BAC–Jan 15 27-P-50.00 GE–Nov 20 26-C-340.00 GE–Nov 20 26-P-340.00 MSFT-Dec 18 26-C-400.00 MSFT–Dec 18 26-P-400.00 SPY–Dec 31 26-C-686.00 SPY–Dec 31 26-P-686.00 MIAX MIAX Sapphire — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Change to the Market for Underlying Security Used for Openings on MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options for Symbol BMNR Effective Thursday, April 9, 2026

title: MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options - Effective for trade dates April 8, 2026 through April 10, 2026 2X OPENING and INTRADAY Valid and Priority Quote Spread Relief in All Symbols
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options - Effective for trade dates April 8, 2026 through April 10, 2026 2X OPENING and INTRADAY Valid and Priority Quote Spread Relief in All Symbols
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Emerald April 08, 2026 | 07:58 AM MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options - Effective for trade dates April 8, 2026 through April 10, 2026 2X OPENING and INTRADAY Valid and Priority Quote Spread Relief in All Symbols Multiplier: 2X Reason: In maintenance of a fair and orderly market. Time: OPENING and INTRADAY Subject Summary: Please be advised, effective for trade dates April 8, 2026 through April 10, 2026, the MIAX Regulatory Department has granted 2 times OPENING and INTRADAY quote parameter relief for all symbols on MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options. Please note, standard quote width is $5 wide, two (2) times width is $10. The quote width listed in the following will be two (2) times the listed width. https://www.miaxglobal.com/markets/us-options/miax-options/market-maker-requirements https://www.miaxglobal.com/markets/us-options/emerald-options/market-maker-requirements For questions or comments, please contact the Regulatory Department at regulatory@miaxglobal.com.

Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Emerald April 07, 2026 | 07:50 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Change to the Market for Underlying Security Used for Openings on MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options for Symbol DCOM Effective Tuesday, April 7, 2026 Dime Community Bancshares, Inc. (“DCOM”) transferred primary listing from the NASDAQ Market to the New York Stock Exchange (“NYSE”) effective today, Tuesday, April 7, 2026. MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options will use NYSE as the “market for the underlying security” for the purpose of Openings on the Exchange. For more information, please refer to the following Regulatory Circulars: MIAX Options Regulatory Circular 2026-48 MIAX Pearl Options Regulatory Circular 2026-48 MIAX Emerald Options Regulatory Circular 2026-37 MIAX Sapphire Options Regulatory Circular 2026-50 Please direct questions to the Regulatory Department at Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities for Q2 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities for Q2 2026
Listing Alert MIAX Options April 06, 2026 | 05:17 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities for Q2 2026 As previously announced in the January 20, 2026 Alert , the MIAX Options Exchanges filed with the SEC to amend the Short Term Option Series Program of each exchange to permit the listing of up to two Monday and Wednesday expirations for options on certain individual stocks or Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF) Shares (“Qualifying Securities”). A Qualifying Security is an individual stock or ETF that, on a quarterly basis: (1) has an underlying security, as measured on the last day of the prior calendar quarter, with: (A) a market capitalization of greater than 700 billion dollars for an individual stock based on the closing price, or (B) Assets under Management (“AUM”) greater than 50 billion dollars for an Exchange-Traded Fund Share based on net asset value (“NAV”); (2) has monthly options volume, as measured by sides traded in the last month preceding the quarter end, of greater than 10 million options; (3) has a position limit of at least 250,000 contracts; and (4) participates in the Penny Interval Program. A Qualifying Security expiration would not be listed on a day when there will be an earnings announcement that takes place after market close. The following securities are Qualified Securities for Q2 2026: Alphabet, Inc. (GOOGL) Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Apple Inc. (AAPL) Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) Each calendar quarter, the above criteria will be reassessed to determine eligibility for the following quarter as a Qualifying Security. The MIAX Option Exchanges will post the list of Qualifying Securities on our website by the close of business on the first trading day of each quarter. Please contact MIAX Listings with any questions at Listings@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7308.

title: MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Reminder: Support for Post Only Order Instruction for Securities Priced Below $1.00 Available Wednesday, April 15, 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl Equities — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Reminder: Support for Post Only Order Instruction for Securities Priced Below $1.00 Available Wednesday, April 15, 2026
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Pearl Equities April 13, 2026 | 10:32 AM MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Reminder: Support for Post Only Order Instruction for Securities Priced Below $1.00 Available Wednesday, April 15, 2026 As previously announced in the April 1, 2026 Alert, the MIAX Pearl Equities (EPRL) Exchange will begin to honor the Post Only Order Instruction for securities priced below $1.00 beginning Wednesday, April 15, 2026. Consistent with current above dollar handling, an order that provides a Post Only Order Instruction in a sub-dollar security will only remove liquidity from the MIAX Pearl Equities book if the dollar value of the trade is greater than or equal to the value of the trade if the member were to add liquidity, inclusive of all fees charged and rebates provided. To determine at the time of a potential execution whether the value of such execution when removing liquidity is greater than or equal to the value of such execution if the order instead posted to the MIAX Pearl Equities book and subsequently added liquidity, the Exchange will use the highest possible rebate paid and highest possible fee charged for such executions on the Exchange. Based on the current MIAX Pearl Equities Fee Schedule, the price improvement gained from executing as a remover must be greater than or equal to 0.35% of the dollar value of trade. Otherwise, the Post Only Order will be posted to the book. Certification is only required for sub-dollar securities if certification has not already been completed for dollar securities. Contact TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com with questions or to arrange certification testing. An application upgrade is not required to use this feature; members will use the existing order entry handling to denote an order as Post Only. FIX Interface: Tag 76 “ExecBroker” MEO Interface: Order Instructions Field “Bit 4 – Post Only” Please refer to the updated MIAX Pearl Equities User Manual found here for additional order book handling information. Further details can be found in the MIAX Pearl Equities RC-2026-04 and related rule filing and SEC approval order. For further information, please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. Regulatory inquiries should be directed to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309. To learn more about MIAX Pearl Equities please contact Sales@miaxglobal.com , (609) 897-8177 or visit miaxglobal.com .

title: MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Reminder: 2026 Market Wide Circuit Breaker Testing on Saturday, April 11, 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl Equities — Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Reminder: 2026 Market Wide Circuit Breaker Testing on Saturday, April 11, 2026
Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Pearl Equities April 08, 2026 | 03:31 PM MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Reminder: 2026 Market Wide Circuit Breaker Testing on Saturday, April 11, 2026 As previously announced in the March 5, 2026 Alert , MIAX Pearl Equities, in coordination with other market centers and the Securities Information Processors (“SIPs”), will participate in the 2026 annual Market Wide Circuit Breaker (“MWCB”) tests. In accordance with MIAX Pearl Equities Rule 2622, Limit Up-Limit Down Plan and Trading Halts, certain MIAX Pearl Equities Members have mandatory participation requirements in the annual MWCB tests. MIAX Pearl Equities will notify Members that are required to participate in the annual MWCB tests prior to the first annual test date. Members must register with Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com prior to testing in order to participate in MWCB tests and are required to submit attestations to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com . Please note, these tests are systematically monitored for exceptions and the results are reviewed on the following Monday, not in real time. Successful testing includes: Receive and process MWCB halt messages from the SIPs; Receive and process resume messages from the SIPs following a MWCB halt; Receive and process market data from the SIPs relevant to MWCB halts; and Send orders following a Level 1 or Level 2 MWCB halt in a manner consistent with usual trading behavior Please refer to MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange Regulatory Circular 2026-02 for more information. Test Dates Saturday, April 11, 2026: Failover / MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #1 Saturday, July 25, 2026: Failover / MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #2 Saturday, September 12, 2026: Dedicated MWCB Level 1, 2 & 3 Saturday, December 5, 2026: Failover / MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #3 For questions regarding the MWCB test, please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. Regulatory inquiries should be directed to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

Trading Alert, Clearing Alert MIAX Futures April 23, 2026 | 03:29 PM MIAX Futures Exchange - Spring Wheat Options Expiration - May 2026 Expiration of May Spring Wheat option positions is scheduled for Friday, April 24, 2026. All Clearing Members should use the MIAX Futures Clearing System (MCS) to update and verify May Spring Wheat option positions by 4:30 PM CT. Clearing Members must submit position information by one of the following: Position Change Submission (PCS) file via SFTP PCS Reporting Specification can be found under Technical Specifications at MIAX Futures Interface Specifications Manually submitting positions to the MIAX Futures Clearing House and verifying in MCS by utilizing the gross position reporting and marking complete. Exception forms to exercise out-of-the money positions or not exercise in-the-money positions, should be submitted by 4:25 PM CT. Firms should expect to see assignments at approximately 5:10 PM CT on April 24, 2026. All account information can be added once option trades have been created. title: MIAX Futures Exchange - Onyx Trading Platform - Financial Futures - Reminder for April 25, 2026 Mock Trading Test
raw_text:
MIAX Futures — Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Futures Exchange - Onyx Trading Platform - Financial Futures - Reminder for April 25, 2026 Mock Trading Test
Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Futures April 23, 2026 | 10:36 AM MIAX Futures Exchange - Onyx Trading Platform - Financial Futures - Reminder for April 25, 2026 Mock Trading Test As previously announced in the February 3, 2026 and March 2, 2026 Alerts, MIAX Futures will conduct a mock trading session on Saturday, April 25, 2026 in preparation for the upcoming launch of Financial Futures on Sunday, May 17, 2026 (Trade Date: May 18, 2026). Once production sessions have been requested and completed, firms may also request to conduct application layer connectivity testing or telnet connectivity testing prior to April 25, 2026 by contacting MIAXfuturestradingoperations@MIAXGlobal.com . Please note, any firms testing through an ISV should confirm proper symbology with their respective ISV prior to testing. Test Day Timeline (ET) : 08:30 a.m. – Pre-opening connectivity windows open 08:45 a.m. – Live Order Window (LOW) First Pre-opening 09:30 a.m. – Extended 1 Trading Session 10:00 a.m. – Regular Trading Session 11:00 a.m. – Extended 2 Trading Session 12:00 p.m. – Closing 12:30 p.m. – Live Order Window (LOW) Closes 1:00 p.m. – System Shutdown Test Day Details : Trade Date will be Saturday, April 25, 2026 All three products will be available for testing from Cloud 1: TBQ (Tini B100 Index Futures) TBX (Tini B500 Index Futures) BX (B500 Index Futures) MIAX Futures industry mock trading testing instruments available upon request MIAX Futures industry mock trading sessions will support the following activity utilizing assigned production network connections: Open firm participation via each of MIAX Futures technical interfaces: Futures Express Interface (FEI) FIX Order Interface (FOI) for FIX orders FIX Drop Copy (FXD) MIAX Futures Market Data Dissemination via each of the MIAX proprietary data feeds: Top of Market Data (ToM) Feed Depth of Market Data (DoM) Feed Firms interested in receiving test data from OCC generated from the Industry Test windows will need to register with OCC one week prior to participation to ensure connectivity and data service setup. OCC will be supporting testing in OCC’s External Test 3 environment for each Industry window. Trades input to MIAX Futures Onyx during the weekend test windows will be played into OCC's External Test 3 environment on each following Monday and will be available for registered entities. To register with OCC for participation, please complete the test request form at www.theocc.com/webapps/cert-test-services and indicate your request relates to MIAX Futures Onyx Industry Testing dates. Testing Suggestions : Send FIX and/or FEI Order Simple and/or Complex STOP Market, STOP Limit, Market, GTD, Market with Protection, STOP Market with Protection Cancel FIX and/or FEI Order Simple and/or Complex Cancel Replace FIX and/or FEI Order Simple and/or Complex Send Mass Cancel Request – this requires Firms to have MFP Access, for industry test #1 we will not have firms able to access this action via MFP Send Complex Strategy Creation Request Receive Execution Simple and/or Complex Participants who plan to Validate Bust and Correction of a Trade via the FIX Drop Copy (FXD) must Coordinate with Trading Operations Market Data Subscribers Validate Instrument Definition Validate Market Data is being received Validate Retransmission Services If you have any questions or need to coordinate testing and connectivity, please contact MIAX Futures Trading Operations at MIAXfuturestradingoperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7303. To revise a current MIAX Alert subscription or to register a new subscription, please visit MIAX Email Subscriptions .

title: MIAX Futures Exchange - Onyx Trading Platform - Financial Futures - April 25, 2026 Mock Trading Test Plan
raw_text:
MIAX Futures — Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Futures Exchange - Onyx Trading Platform - Financial Futures - April 25, 2026 Mock Trading Test Plan
Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Futures April 15, 2026 | 11:09 AM MIAX Futures Exchange - Onyx Trading Platform - Financial Futures - April 25, 2026 Mock Trading Test Plan As previously announced in the February 3, 2026 and March 2, 2026 Alerts, MIAX Futures will conduct a mock trading session on Saturday, April 25, 2026 in preparation for the upcoming launch of Financial Futures on Sunday, May 17, 2026 (Trade Date: May 18, 2026). Once production sessions have been requested and completed, firms may also request to conduct application layer connectivity testing or telnet connectivity testing prior to April 25, 2026 by contacting MIAXfuturestradingoperations@MIAXGlobal.com . Please note, any firms testing through an ISV should confirm proper symbology with their respective ISV prior to testing. Test Day Timeline (ET) : 08:30 a.m. – Pre-opening connectivity windows open 08:45 a.m. – Live Order Window (LOW) First Pre-opening 09:30 a.m. – Extended 1 Trading Session 10:00 a.m. – Regular Trading Session 11:00 a.m. – Extended 2 Trading Session 12:00 p.m. – Closing 12:30 p.m. – Live Order Window (LOW) Closes 1:00 p.m. – System Shutdown Test Day Details : Trade Date will be Saturday, April 25, 2026 All three products will be available for testing from Cloud 1: TBQ (Tini B100 Index Futures) TBX (Tini B500 Index Futures) BX (B500 Index Futures) MIAX Futures industry mock trading testing instruments available upon request MIAX Futures industry mock trading sessions will support the following activity utilizing assigned production network connections: Open firm participation via each of MIAX Futures technical interfaces: Futures Express Interface (FEI) FIX Order Interface (FOI) for FIX orders FIX Drop Copy (FXD) MIAX Futures Market Data Dissemination via each of the MIAX proprietary data feeds: Top of Market Data (ToM) Feed Depth of Market Data (DoM) Feed Firms interested in receiving test data from OCC generated from the Industry Test windows will need to register with OCC one week prior to participation to ensure connectivity and data service setup. OCC will be supporting testing in OCC’s External Test 3 environment for each Industry window. Trades input to MIAX Futures Onyx during the weekend test windows will be played into OCC's External Test 3 environment on each following Monday and will be available for registered entities. To register with OCC for participation, please complete the test request form at www.theocc.com/webapps/cert-test-services and indicate your request relates to MIAX Futures Onyx Industry Testing dates. Testing Suggestions : Send FIX and/or FEI Order Simple and/or Complex STOP Market, STOP Limit, Market, GTD, Market with Protection, STOP Market with Protection Cancel FIX and/or FEI Order Simple and/or Complex Cancel Replace FIX and/or FEI Order Simple and/or Complex Send Mass Cancel Request – this requires Firms to have MFP Access, for industry test #1 we will not have firms able to access this action via MFP Send Complex Strategy Creation Request Receive Execution Simple and/or Complex Participants who plan to Validate Bust and Correction of a Trade via the FIX Drop Copy (FXD) must Coordinate with Trading Operations Market Data Subscribers Validate Instrument Definition Validate Market Data is being received Validate Retransmission Services If you have any questions or need to coordinate testing and connectivity, please contact MIAX Futures Trading Operations at MIAXfuturestradingoperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7303. To revise a current MIAX Alert subscription or to register a new subscription, please visit MIAX Email Subscriptions .

title: MIAX Futures Exchange - Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat - Discontinuance of Warehouse Receipts for Delivery Against Futures
raw_text:
MIAX Futures — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert, Clearing Alert, Product Alert — MIAX Futures Exchange - Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat - Discontinuance of Warehouse Receipts for Delivery Against Futures
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert, Clearing Alert, Product Alert MIAX Futures April 08, 2026 | 03:18 PM MIAX Futures Exchange - Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat - Discontinuance of Warehouse Receipts for Delivery Against Futures As previously announced and approved by the CFTC, physical delivery in satisfaction of Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat (HRSW) Futures will change from warehouse receipts to shipping certificates beginning with the September 2026 delivery period. Effective July 24, 2026, for the September 2026 and subsequent contracts, warehouse receipts will not be deliverable against Minneapolis HRSW Futures. Physical delivery in satisfaction of Minneapolis HRSW Futures for September 2026 and subsequent contracts will be by shipping certificate only. After the conclusion of the July 2026 delivery period on July 23, 2026, holders of outstanding warehouse receipts issued prior to the September 2026 contract may: Sell the warehouse receipt back to the issuing warehouse at a mutually agreeable price; Cancel the warehouse receipt for load-out; Continue holding the warehouse receipt and accrue storage obligations to the issuing warehouse; Sell, transfer, or otherwise dispose of such receipts as permitted by the United States Warehouse Act and applicable law. Effective with the September 2026 contract, clearing members will be able to register shipping certificates on behalf of regular elevators for delivery against Minneapolis HRSW Futures. Additional information on shipping certificates can be found in the FAQ here . If you have any questions, please contact MIAX Futures Market Regulation at MIAXFuturesMarketReg@miaxglobal.com . Additional updates will be provided through future Alerts.

raw_text:
NYSE American Options and NYSE Arca Options: MSCI Index Options Available for Trading on May 1, 2026 - MXUSA, MXWLD, MXACW
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Effective May 1, 2026, options on the MSCI USA Index (MXUSA), MSCI World Index (MXWLD), and MSCI ACWI Index (MXACW) will be listed on NYSE American Options and NYSE Arca Options - expanding the suite of global index options available to market participants. This addition builds on ICE's decades-long partnership with MSCI, which includes providing the world's most liquid MSCI futures markets and reflects a continued commitment to offering clients greater choice and flexibility across global benchmarks. For additional information, please visit the NYSE MSCI Global Index Options webpage or contact rmteam@nyse.com.

title: NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol PS
raw_text:
NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol PS
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading
NYSE has determined that the Common Stock issued by Pershing Square Inc. is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker PS with CUSIP 71531U102 on April 29, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security PS until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading. Pershing Square Inc (NYSE: PS) is distributed without additional consideration to investors in the IPO of Pershing Square USA, Ltd. (NYSE: PSUS), closed‑end fund, with pricing anticipated on April 28, 2026 and listing on April 29, 2026. The two securities will trade separately.

title: 2026 Regulation SCI BCP/DR Testing - October 24, 2026
raw_text:
2026 Regulation SCI BCP/DR Testing - October 24, 2026
Markets: NYSE, NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES, NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS, NYSE BONDS, FINRA/NYSE TRF, NYSE NATIONAL, NYSE TEXAS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
On October 24, 2026, NYSE, NYSE American, NYSE American Options, NYSE Arca, NYSE Arca Options, NYSE Bonds, NYSE Texas, NYSE National, and FINRA/NYSE TRF® (collectively, "NYSE") will conduct business continuity and disaster recovery testing during the SIFMA coordinated industry test. Market participants that meet criteria established by the NYSE exchanges for designation as a required participant ("Required Participants") will be notified by NYSE in April 2026. In preparation for the October 24th test, the NYSE will make the Cermak Disaster Recovery ("DR") environments available for connectivity testing on September 26, 2026 and October 10, 2026 .The test hours for the two pre-tests and the test itself are 8:30am to 12:00pm ET. Testing Requirements Participating firms, including all Required Participants, must confirm participation (whether successful or not) by: Connecting to NYSE's Cermak DR site from the firm's Production or DR site; Sending an order or quote (NYSE will route to participating exchanges during the test); Testing ability to receive market data; and Completing an NYSE RegSCI DR Test - Firm Participation Form ("Checklist") The Checklist will be provided to participating firms in advance of the test. Upon receipt of the Checklist, NYSE will provide firms with a "confirmation of test completion" email to a contact provided by the firm on the Checklist. Required Participants Required Participants will be designated pursuant to the criteria set forth below, which are intended to identify the firms that, taken as a whole, represent the minimum necessary for the maintenance of fair and orderly markets in the event of the activation of business continuity and disaster recovery plans: Required Participants for the NYSE, NYSE American, NYSE Arca, NYSE Texas and NYSE National equities markets are (i) each firm that executed 3 % or more of the applicable exchange's aggregate daily volume over a three-month period and (ii) all firms that are active Lead Market Makers (LMM) on NYSE Arca, Designated Market Makers (DMM) on NYSE, electronic Designated Market Makers (e-DMMs) on NYSE American, or Supplemental Liquidity Providers (SLP/SLMM) on NYSE. Required Participants for the NYSE American Options and NYSE Arca Options markets are (i) each firm (ATP or OTP holders) that executed 3 % or greater of the applicable exchange's aggregate electronic average daily volume over a three-month period and (ii) all firms that are Lead Market Makers (LMM) on NYSE Arca Options or Specialists/E-Specialists on NYSE American Options. Required Participants for the Bonds market are each member organization that executed 20% or more of the market's aggregate daily volume over a three-month period. NYSE will announce any further modifications to Required Participant criteria by Trader Update and will designate and notify Required Participants at least three months in advance of the scheduled test. Additionally, all NYSE exchanges mandate that SCI Required Participants also participate in one Market Wide Circuit Breaker Test within the year. The dates of the 2026 MWCB tests are April 11, July 25, September 12, and December 5, 2026. Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions regarding this update.

title: NYSE Arca Options - NYSE Pillar Hardware Upgrade
raw_text:
NYSE Arca Options - NYSE Pillar Hardware Upgrade
Markets: NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
On June 1, 2026 , NYSE Arca Options will upgrade hardware for its NYSE Pillar technology platform. The migration will be an immediate cutover, with no parallel production period. There are no changes to specifications or sessions with this upgrade. The certification environment will be available beginning April 27, 2026 . Proprietary Market Data Feeds NYSE Arca Options Top (BBO and Trades), Deep and Complex (excluding Imbalance and Summary) feeds will increase from 17 to 24 Publisher, Refresh and Retransmission channels. NYSE Arca Options Certification Source IPs will be changing with the hardware upgrade. Please refer to NYSE Proprietary Data IP Addresses for new Certification source IPs. New Production channel information will be provided in a future trader update. Customers should use the start of day symbol reference data for symbol mapping information. There will be no XDP format changes. Key Dates Certification environment available Monday, April 27, 2026 Industry Test #1 Saturday, May 16, 2026 Industry Test #2 Saturday, May 30, 2026 New Hardware in Production Monday, June 1, 2026 Market Data channel and DR hardware changes will be announced at a later date. Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this update.

venue: NYSE American Options / NYSE Arca Options
title: NYSE Arca Options and NYSE American Options – Monday / Wednesday Expirations for Qualifying Securities
raw_text:
NYSE Arca Options and NYSE American Options – Monday / Wednesday Expirations for Qualifying Securities
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
NYSE American Options and NYSE Arca Options are expanding the Short Term Options Series Program to permit the listing of up to two Monday and Wednesday expirations in addition to the Friday weekly expiration for Qualifying Securities. To qualify, a security must meet the following requirements on a quarterly basis: Individual stocks must have a market capitalization exceeding $700 billion (based on closing price), or Exchange Traded Funds (ETF) must have Assets under Management (AUM) exceeding $50 billion (based on net asset value) Additionally, Monthly options volume exceeding 10 million contracts (measured by sides traded in the final month of the preceding quarter) A position limit of at least 250,000 contracts, and Participation in the Penny Interval Program Each calendar quarter, the Exchange will evaluate individual stocks and ETFs to determine eligibility for the following quarter as a Qualifying Security. The Exchange will make the list of Qualifying Securities available by close of business on the first trading day of the quarter. Qualifying Securities that do not continue to meet the above criteria would no longer be permitted to list Monday and Wednesday expiries beginning on the second day of the following quarter. Qualifying Securities for Q2 2026 Security Name Symbol Apple Inc. AAPL Amazon.Com Inc AMZN Broadcom Inc. AVGO Alphabet Inc. GOOGL iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF IBIT Meta Platforms, Inc.
